name: Buffalo Wings and Blue Cheese Dressing
recipeInstructions:
For the Chicken Wings:
1. Heat oil to a temperature of 375°F in a deep-sided pot.
2. Rinse chicken wings and pat dry on paper towels.
3. Mix melted butter with Tabasco sauce, set aside.
4. Fry chicken wings in batches until golden brown (10-12 minutes).
5. Drain wings on paper towels and repeat if needed.
6. Keep wings warm at 200°F in a single layer.
For the Dressing:
1. Combine blue cheese, mayonnaise, sour cream or yogurt, black pepper, lemon juice, and vinegar in a small mixing bowl.
2. Break up cheese with a fork until smooth.
3. To serve, place wings on a platter or plates and serve with dressing and celery sticks. Makes 36 pieces. The dressing can be prepared up to two days in advance and refrigerated.
